name: Sicilian Fish Stew
description: From The Complete Mediterranean Cookbook (ATK), p.267. Swordfish simmered in a Sicilian sweet-savory tomato broth with celery, raisins, capers; finished with a pine-nut, mint, and orange-zest gremolata. Serve with crusty bread.
recipeIngredient: 2 lb skinless swordfish steaks, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 (28-oz) can whole peeled tomatoes, drained (juice reserved) and chopped
recipeInstructions: Mix pine nuts, mint, a quarter of the garlic, and orange zest for serving.
Soften onion + celery, add the remaining garlic, thyme, and pepper flakes.
Add wine + reserved tomato juice and reduce by half; add tomatoes, clam juice, raisins, and capers; simmer ~15 min.
Nestle in the seasoned swordfish, simmer 4 min, then off heat covered 3 min.
Serve sprinkled with the pine nut mixture.

ai.cliffNotes: Build a tomato-based broth with wine reduction and clam juice, then gently poach swordfish in it while finishing with a bright pine nut-mint gremolata for textural and flavor contrast.
keyTips: Reduce wine and tomato juice by half before adding the broth to concentrate flavor and cook off alcohol
Drain canned tomatoes and reserve juice separately—this gives you control over liquid concentration rather than adding watery tomato liquid
Toast pine nuts and keep the gremolata separate until serving; this preserves their crunch and the mint's brightness
Pull the pan off heat and cover after 4 minutes of simmering the fish—residual heat finishes cooking without overcooking delicate swordfish
